name: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
summary: A novel of the Jazz Age, The Great Gatsby tells the story of the mysterious millionaire Jay Gatsby and his obsession with the beautiful Daisy Buchanan.

name: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
summary: A novel about the serious issues of rape and racial inequality, told through the eyes of young Scout Finch in the Deep South during the 1930s.

author: George Orwell
summary: A dystopian novel set in a totalitarian society under constant surveillance, where independent thought is persecuted as a 'thoughtcrime'.

name: The Catcher in the Rye
author: J.D. Salinger
summary: The story of Holden Caulfield, a teenage boy who has been expelled from prep school and his journey through New York City.

name: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
summary: A romantic novel that charts the emotional development of Elizabeth Bennet, who learns about the repercussions of hasty judgments and the true nature of love.

name: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
summary: A fantasy novel that follows the quest of home-loving hobbit Bilbo Baggins to win a share of the treasure guarded by Smaug the dragon.

name: Moby Dick
author: Herman Melville
summary: The narrative of Captain Ahab's obsessive quest to kill Moby Dick, the giant white sperm whale.

name: War and Peace
author: Leo Tolstoy
summary: A historical novel that chronicles the French invasion of Russia and the impact of the Napoleonic era on Tsarist society.

name: The Art of War
author: Sun Tzu
summary: An ancient Chinese military treatise detailing psychological warfare and strategy that remains influential today.

name: The Road
author: Cormac McCarthy
summary: A post-apocalyptic novel that tells the story of a father and son's journey through a burned America.

name: The Book Thief
author: Markus Zusak
summary: A story narrated by Death about a young girl living in Nazi Germany, who finds solace by stealing books and sharing them with others.

name: Jane Eyre
author: Charlotte Brontë
summary: A classic novel that follows the experiences of its eponymous heroine, including her growth to adulthood and her love for Mr. Rochester.

name: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley
summary: A dystopian novel set in a futuristic World State, where citizens are environmentally engineered into an intelligence-based social hierarchy.

name: The Da Vinci Code
author: Dan Brown
summary: A mystery thriller novel that follows Robert Langdon and Sophie Neveu as they investigate a murder in the Louvre Museum.

name: The Girl on the Train
author: Paula Hawkins
summary: A psychological thriller that tells the story of Rachel Watson, who becomes entangled in a missing persons investigation.

name: The Hunger Games
author: Suzanne Collins
summary: A dystopian novel set in a post-apocalyptic future where children are chosen to fight to the death in a televised event.

name: The Shining
author: Stephen King
summary: A family stays in an isolated hotel where a sinister presence influences the father into violence, while his psychic son sees terrifying visions of the past.

name: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
summary: On a desert planet, a young nobleman becomes the leader of a rebellion to protect the galaxy’s most valuable resource while fulfilling a dangerous prophecy.

name: The Little Prince
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry
summary: A stranded pilot meets a young prince from another planet who shares profound observations about life, love, and the strange nature of the adult world.

name: The Chronicles of Narnia
author: C.S. Lewis
summary: Four siblings discover a magical wardrobe that leads to a frozen land where they must join a Great Lion to defeat an evil White Witch.

name: Frankenstein
author: Mary Shelley
summary: A scientist creates life from scavenged body parts, only to be horrified by his creation, leading to a tragic cycle of revenge and social isolation.

name: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
summary: A shepherd boy travels to Egypt to find a hidden treasure, discovering that the journey's wisdom is the true prize.

name: Dracula
author: Bram Stoker
summary: An ancient vampire moves from Transylvania to England to spread his undead curse, while a small group of heroes fights to stop him.

name: The Martian
author: Andy Weir
summary: An astronaut is accidentally left behind on Mars and must use his scientific knowledge to survive until a rescue mission can reach him.

name: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
summary: A destitute student commits a murder to test a theory, only to be consumed by guilt and the psychological pressure of a clever investigator.

name: Life of Pi
author: Yann Martel
summary: After a shipwreck, a young boy survives on a lifeboat in the Pacific Ocean with a Bengal tiger for 227 days.

name: The Odyssey
author: Homer
summary: The hero Odysseus struggles to return home to Ithaca after the Trojan War, facing gods, monsters, and sirens along the way.

name: Great Expectations
author: Charles Dickens
summary: An orphan named Pip receives a mysterious fortune from an unknown benefactor and tries to become a gentleman to win the heart of Estella.

name: The Picture of Dorian Gray
author: Oscar Wilde
summary: A young man remains youthful while his portrait ages and reflects his moral decay and sinful life.

name: Slaughterhouse-Five
author: Kurt Vonnegut
summary: An American soldier becomes 'unstuck in time,' experiencing his life and the firebombing of Dresden in a non-linear, satirical fashion.

name: The Grapes of Wrath
author: John Steinbeck
summary: During the Great Depression, a poor family of sharecroppers travels to California in search of work, dignity, and a better future.

name: Fahrenheit 451
author: Ray Bradbury
summary: In a future where books are banned and burned, a fireman begins to question the state and joins a secret group of book-preservers.

name: The Handmaid's Tale
author: Margaret Atwood
summary: In a near-future theocracy, a woman is forced into reproductive servitude and must navigate a dangerous world to find her lost daughter.

name: A Game of Thrones
author: George R.R. Martin
summary: Noble families vie for control of the Iron Throne in a land where winters last years and ancient threats return from the north.

name: The Count of Monte Cristo
author: Alexandre Dumas
summary: Wrongfully imprisoned, a man escapes and uses a hidden treasure to meticulously destroy the lives of the men who betrayed him.

name: Neuromancer
author: William Gibson
summary: A washed-up computer hacker is hired for one last job that leads him into a global conspiracy within a high-tech virtual reality.

name: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
summary: A brief history of humankind, exploring how biology and history have defined us and enhanced our understanding of society.
